subject: science

primary.justification: The chapter's whole domain is atomic structure via the Bohr model and the combining capacity (valency) that follows from electronic configuration — the 'atomic structure (Bohr's model) and properties (valency)' clause of C-1.1. The periodic-table / compound-formation clause of C-1.1 is not developed here; it is the domain of the sibling chapter (Atomic Foundation of Matter). The chapter's four sub-topics (model evolution, atomic/mass number and electronic configuration, valency, isotopes/isobars) are all facets of atomic structure and serve this single competency; no distinct second strand exists within CG-1 (mixtures C-1.2 and chemical equations C-1.3 are absent).

dissolution_test: This chapter builds the student's ability to determine the subatomic composition, electronic configuration, and valency of an atom by reconstructing how successive experimental evidence (cathode rays, gold-foil scattering, neutron discovery) reshaped atomic models from Thomson to Bohr, and by applying the 2n² shell-filling rule and the octet rule to predict combining capacity.
